title: -Reject All Bids Associated with Cesar Chavez Park Perimeter Pathway Improvement Project

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution rejecting all bids for the work associated with the Cesar Chavez
Park Perimeter Pathway Improvement Project (Bid Specification No. 25-11702-C) in
order to return bidder proposal guarantees.
sections.current_situation:
The City conducted a competitive Invitation For Bid (IFB) process in March, 2025, and
opened bids on April 17, 2025. The City received 5 bids from a low bid of $742,980.79
to a high bid $1,143,708.00 for the base bid work. Bay Cities Paving & Grading was the
apparent low bidder.
An Off-Agenda Memo to Council was issued on June 5, 20251 related to the
environmental compliance updates at former landfill at Cesar Chavez Park. As a result,
the construction phase of the Cesar Chavez Park Perimeter Pathway Improvement
Project is paused.
sections.background:
In FY2022, the State of California allocated a one-time distribution of surplus state funds
to cities and counties throughout California. Berkeley received a one-time allocation of
$15 million for Berkeley Waterfront improvement projects and the State Coastal
Conservancy (SCC) was selected to administer these funds. On December 13, 2022,
the City Council approved the execution of five grant contracts with SCC for five

Cesar Chavez Park Perimeter Pathway Improvement Project September 9, 2025
improvement projects at the Berkeley Waterfront and began the design and construction
phases (Resolution No. 70,629-N.S.). The Cesar Chavez Park Perimeter Pathway
improvement project was one of the five projects.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects, climate impacts, or sustainability
opportunities associated with the subject of this report.
sections.rationale:
Due to ongoing environmental monitoring and compliance activities at Cesar Chavez
Park, the Project is paused at the design phase.
sections.alternatives:
None

sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
REJECT ALL BIDS FOR WORK ASSOCIATED WITH THE CESAR CHAVEZ PARK
PERIMETER PATHWAY PROJECT
WHEREAS, the Cesar Chavez Perimeter Pathway is a popular recreational trail for the
Berkeley Waterfront, serving the general public, and the amenities at Cesar Chavez
Park; and
WHEREAS, the pavement condition at the perimeter has deteriorated over recent
years, causing undesirable trail surface for users; and
WHEREAS, the City conducted a competitive Invitation For Bid (IFB) process in March,
2025, and opened bids on April 17, 2025. The City received five bids, the lowest being
from Bay Cities Paving and Grading; and
WHEREAS, due to ongoing environmental monitoring and compliance activities at
Cesar Chavez Park, the Project is paused at the design phase.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that all
bids are rejected for the work associated with the Cesar Chavez Park Perimeter
Pathway Improvement Project (Bid Specification No. 25-11702-C) in order to return
bidder proposal guarantees.
